title: A comprehensive guide to the 2026 Oscar for production design, the winner and nominees
text: Director Guillermo del Toro's Netflix adaptation of Frankenstein won the award for its theatrical approach to Mary Shelley's classic tale. Production designer Tamara Deverell created an operatic visual world featuring Victor Frankenstein's patina-covered laboratory in an abandoned water tower, a fully-constructed Arctic exploration ship, and his ancestral estate shot across English and Scottish locations including Wilton House and Burghley House. Deverell explained: My approach to the overall design was more theatrical than I had ever done with emphasis on sculpted elements and textured finishes. Sinners featured Hannah Beachler's design for Ryan Coogler's vampire horror film building a Jim Crow-era Mississippi world with supernatural elements. Hamnet featured Fiona Crombie reimagining the Globe Theatre as an intimate, tree-like wooden venue. Marty Supreme featured Jack Fisk recreating 1950s New York. One Battle After Another featured Florencia Martin crafting Paul Thomas Anderson's California thriller.
